content.text: assistant-ui is a headless React UI library for AI chat applications. It provides composable primitives —

— that handle streaming, optimistic updates, and tool call rendering, while leaving all visual styling to you. The library works with any AI SDK-compatible backend and has first-class support for Claude via the Anthropic AI SDK adapter.

attrs.alt: assistant-ui component hierarchy: AssistantRuntimeProvider wraps Thread, which contains Thread.Viewport (messages), makeAssistantTool renderers, and Composer

content.text:
npm install @assistant-ui/react @assistant-ui/react-ai-sdk ai @ai-sdk/anthropic
# or for a direct Anthropic backend:
npm install @assistant-ui/react
pip install anthropic fastapi uvicorn   # backend
ai + @ai-sdk/anthropic — if using the Vercel AI SDK data stream protocol on the backend
Quick Start: Thread with Edge Runtime
with a runtime adapter and a
component. The runtime adapter handles the backend communication;
, the backend is a POST endpoint that returns the Vercel AI SDK data stream protocol. The AI SDK's
Without the AI SDK, you can implement the data stream protocol directly in FastAPI. The protocol is text/plain with SSE-style prefix encoding:

# server.py — FastAPI backend for assistant-ui (without AI SDK)
# assistant-ui speaks the Vercel AI SDK data stream protocol:
# each SSE line is: "data: <json>\n\n" or "0:<text>\n\n" for text deltas
import anthropic, json, uuid
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel

app    = FastAPI()
client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()

class ChatRequest(BaseModel):
    messages: list[dict]
    system:   str = ""

@app.post("/api/chat")
async def chat(req: ChatRequest):
    async def stream():
        # assistant-ui data stream protocol: prefix "0:" for text, "d:" for done
        async with client.messages.stream(
            model="claude-opus-4-8",
            max_tokens=4096,
            system=req.system or "You are a helpful assistant.",
            messages=req.messages,
        ) as s:
            async for text in s.text_stream:
                # escape the text as a JSON string value
                yield f"0:{json.dumps(text)}\n"

            # finishReason signal
            yield f'd:{{"finishReason":"stop","usage":{{}}}}\n'

    return StreamingResponse(stream(), media_type="text/plain;charset=utf-8",
                             headers={"x-vercel-ai-data-stream": "v1"})
works out of the box but applies its own styles. For full design control, compose the headless primitives:
All primitives are unstyled — apply Tailwind classes directly on each element
Thread.Viewport auto-scrolls to new messages; Thread.ScrollToBottom renders the jump-to-bottom button
Composer.Input auto-grows as the user types (textarea with min-height: auto)
Swap CustomUserMessage / CustomAssistantMessage via the components prop on Thread.Messages

connects a Zod schema to a React render function. The render prop is called while the model streams args (status.type === 'running') and again after the tool result arrives — enabling a skeleton → real component swap identical to the raw streaming pattern, but with the schema and component colocated.
assistant-ui exports hooks for reading and mutating thread state from any component inside the
tree. Use these to build custom controls or attach analytics.
provides a purely in-process runtime for tests. You supply an
synchronously — no server, no SSE parsing, no timers needed.

assistant-ui eliminates the lowest-level plumbing of AI chat UIs: scroll management, streaming text injection, tool call lifecycle, and optimistic message rendering. The headless component model means you never fight against default styles — every visual decision stays in Tailwind classes on your own components. The

primitive colocates the JSON Schema, Zod validation, and React render for each tool in one place, making generative UI maintainable as the tool library grows. For production, pair assistant-ui with a FastAPI or Next.js Edge backend that returns the AI SDK data stream protocol, and validate all tool args with Zod before passing them to render components.
